backlogItem: B-037 (Pi Sprint phase continued: deploy-state gaps + sync restoration from Spool's live-system test) + TD-035 (SIGTERM responsiveness) + Spool parallel deliverables tracked in sprintNotes.

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-17 close baseline. Sprint 17 shipped US-220-225 (+25 new tests per Ralph handoff) onto Sprint 16's 3079 baseline plus a few post-sprint fixes (requirements-pi.txt pygame removal + TD-035 filing + deploy-script fixes). Fast suite ~3135 approx; verify at sprint start. Sprint 17 merged to main@1ee4c2b 2026-04-22. Post-sprint: main@7b4a503 (pygame fix), main@a6bfa2f (TD-035 filing).

sprintNotes: GOAL: close the deploy-state gaps Spool's post-Sprint-17 live-system test surfaced, restore Pi->server sync (stranded since 2026-04-19), fix the drive_summary + drive_end bugs that kept Drive 3 from being cleanly captured, harden the server + journald deploy paths. Drive 3 proved the Pi capture surface works (engine graded EXCELLENT); Sprint 18 closes the gap from 'captured' to 'usable across the full pipeline'.

EXECUTION ORDER (P0s sequenced, P1/P2 parallel-safe): US-226 (sync, M, anchor) -> US-227 (truncate, S, deps US-226). After both P0s: US-228 / US-229 / US-230 / US-231 all independent (no cross-deps). US-232 (TD-035 SIGTERM) and US-233 (pre-mint orphan backfill) are P2 opportunistic.

THEME: Sprint 17 closed Spool's aged safety debt + finished Sprint 16 integration + shipped deeper stage wiring. Drive 3's post-deploy test surfaced EXCELLENT engine health (thermostat opens clean at 80C, LTFT quantized -6.25%, STFT active closed-loop, O2 stoich 0.04-0.92V, timing 4-18 BTDC warmup progression) but exposed deploy-state gaps nobody caught during Sprint 17 validation: sync broken 4 days, drive_summary NULL on cold-start, drive_end not firing, journald empty, server unmanaged. This sprint closes those gaps.

CONSTRAINTS: US-227 (truncate) MUST run AFTER US-226 (sync) ships + Drive 3's 3272 rows are confirmed landed on server. US-228 preserves US-206 warm-restart-NULL rule (ambient_temp_at_start_c=NULL on warm restart stays). US-229 does NOT stop ELM_VOLTAGE polling (it's a legitimate battery monitor) -- only filters drive-end signal source. US-231 mirrors US-210 pattern exactly for server tier (Restart=always, User=mcornelison, EnvironmentFile). US-232 preserves US-211 backoff schedule.
GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool consolidated note 2026-04-23 (primary); TD-035 file + Sprint 17 post-deploy pygame investigation; US-210 / US-206 / US-211 / US-200 shipped artifacts.

SPOOL PARALLEL DELIVERABLES (tracked here, NOT Ralph stories): (a) First real-drive review ritual execution against Drive 3 after US-226 sync lands -- Spool runs US-219 ritual, grades Ollama output against DESIGN_NOTE gates, may propose prompt iteration. (b) Update knowledge.md 'Real Vehicle Data' section -- Drive 3 supersedes Session 23 as authoritative warm-idle baseline (thermostat opens 80C, LTFT -6.25% quantized, timing 4-18 BTDC progression, full 20C->80C warmup curve). Post-sync deliverable so drive_id=3 is referenceable server-side. (c) DSM DTC interpretation cheat sheet -- now unblocked by US-204 Sprint 15. Post-drive docs work. (d) TD-033 telemetry_logger <-> UpsMonitor audit -- 20-min Spool deliverable.

ALSO TO VERIFY (not a story): 1853 physics_sim rows in Pi drive_id=2 from 2026-04-23 03:12-03:14 UTC -- Ralph or CIO confirms whether this was an intentional benchtest (good signal US-212 is tagging correctly) or unintentional leakage. No story unless finding demands one.

POST-SPRINT-18 PAYOFF: full Pi->server pipeline confirmed working; Drive 3 landed on server; Spool's review ritual runs end-to-end; drive_summary + drive_end bugs fixed for future drives; journald persists across Pi reboots; server auto-restarts on crash/reboot; SIGTERM-responsive collector makes future systemctl restart calls take seconds not 90s.

stories.title: Restore Pi->server sync -- last success 2026-04-19; Drive 3 stranded on Pi
stories.intent: Pi sync_log shows last successful push at 2026-04-19T13:48:05Z (the Session 23 149-row test). Four days later, Drive 3 (2026-04-23, ~9.5 min, 3272 rows of graded-EXCELLENT real telemetry) sits on the Pi only; chi-srv-01 obd2db is empty. Network is fine (HTTP 200 to /docs), server uvicorn up, sync client config keys present in config.json -- but the sync never runs. Spool's triage shortlist: (a) config key the sync client reads may have been renamed/dropped post-US-213 migration-gate refactor; top-level pi.sync is missing. (b) sync trigger may be drive_end-bound and drive_end isn't firing (US-229 separately). (c) orchestrator init path may have dropped the sync-client component during Sprint 15/16 refactor. This is THE sprint anchor -- Chi-Srv-01 is decorative without it; US-219 review ritual, AI analytics, baseline calibration all blocked.

stories.title: Pi + server operational truncate (Sprint 18 edition) -- 2.9M stale drive_id=1 rows
stories.intent: Pi realtime_data holds 2,939,278 rows tagged data_source='real' in drive_id=1, span 2026-04-21 02:27 to 2026-04-23 03:12 UTC. Car was not running during that 49-hour window -- these rows are pre-US-212-hygiene pollution, almost certainly from simulator/benchtest leaks before the tagging fix took effect. Mirrors US-205's Session 23 truncate exactly. MUST run AFTER US-226 restores sync so Drive 3's legitimate 3272 rows are preserved via server propagation before the local truncate. Also includes 225 pre-mint orphan rows from Drive 3 (NULL drive_id from 39-sec BT-connect-to-drive-start window) -- those stay (US-233 addresses separately), scope here is only drive_id=1 garbage + drive_counter reset.

stories.title: Fix US-206 drive_summary NULL on cold-start -- timing bug drive_start before first IAT reading
stories.intent: Drive 3 (2026-04-23) shipped a drive_summary row with NULL for all three sensor fields (ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, barometric_kpa_at_start) even though those values ARE present in realtime_data for the same drive. Root cause: drive_start fires before first IAT/BATTERY_V/BARO readings arrive from ECU; the SummaryRecorder captures the snapshot early, sees no values, writes NULL, and doesn't backfill. Bug breaks US-206's whole purpose -- ambient/baro/battery context per drive is the anchor for Spool's interpretation layer. Two fix options: (a) defer drive_summary INSERT until first IAT reading captured, (b) UPDATE the row when first matching readings arrive post-INSERT. Ralph picks + tests.

tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_summary_cold_start.py (new -- simulate drive_start with empty snapshot, assert chosen strategy: either INSERT is deferred OR subsequent UPDATE fills NULL fields)
tests/pi/integration/test_drive_summary_backfill.py (new -- full drive_start -> first IAT arrives -> drive_summary row eventually has non-null values)

If ECU doesn't provide a value within the timeout (e.g., baro on 2G Eclipse), that field stays NULL -- explicit policy, documented.

stories.title: Fix drive_end not firing -- ELM_VOLTAGE heartbeat keeps drive open past engine-off
stories.intent: Drive 3 engine-off at 16:46:21 UTC but NO drive_end event ever fired in connection_log. Drive_detector keys on 'rows still arriving' to infer engine state -- but ELM_VOLTAGE (adapter-level PID, not ECU-dependent) kept ticking for 6+ minutes after engine-off, so the detector thought the drive was still active. Drive 3 is 'effectively still open right now' per Spool. Compounds US-226 if sync is drive_end-bound -- but US-226 will add interval-bound sync as defensive measure, so this bug's downstream impact is the ANALYTICS trigger (per-drive stats computed at drive_end). Fix: filter drive-end detection on ECU-sourced PIDs only; ignore ELM_VOLTAGE and any other adapter-level heartbeat.

stories.title: Journald persistence deploy hardening -- verify /var/log/journal populated post-deploy
stories.intent: US-210 shipped /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/99-obd-persistent.conf with Storage=persistent, but Spool's post-deploy audit found /var/log/journal/ EMPTY on the Pi. Systemd-journald never picked up the new config -- either the restart didn't fire in the deploy step, failed silently, or journal is being written to /run/log/journal (tmpfs) still. Fix: deploy-pi.sh step_install_journald_persistent adds a post-check that verifies /var/log/journal/<machine-id>/ exists after the systemctl restart systemd-journald call. If missing, fail deploy loudly instead of proceeding silently. Also add acceptance test that SSH-verifies persistent journal after a fresh deploy. Downstream impact: next Pi crash without persistent journal = log evidence gone (the exact pattern Session 6 UPS drain hit); also blocks US-216 orchestrator forensic testing.

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: SSH Pi; verify /var/log/journal/ state (likely still empty); confirm /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/99-obd-persistent.conf is present; systemd-journald active.

deploy/deploy-pi.sh step_install_journald_persistent: after systemctl restart systemd-journald, the function verifies /var/log/journal/ has a machine-id subdir AND journalctl --disk-usage > 0. If either check fails, print diagnostic + exit non-zero.
Diagnostic output on failure includes: `journalctl --disk-usage`, `ls /var/log/journal/`, `journalctl --verify`, `cat /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf`, `systemctl is-active systemd-journald`.
Live deploy test: run deploy-pi.sh against the Pi; confirm journald now persists; verify via `ls /var/log/journal/<machine-id>/` returns files.

If initial live-deploy shows journal still empty (existing Pi state), Ralph files inbox note with the diagnostic output and PROPOSES the recovery path (likely: mkdir /var/log/journal + restart journald) -- do NOT silently create the directory without documenting the recovery rationale.

verification: bash deploy/deploy-pi.sh  (full deploy, verify step prints the acceptance check)
invariants: Fail LOUDLY on verification failure. Silent pass-through is what caused this bug in the first place.
Do NOT mkdir /var/log/journal as a silent recovery. If it's missing, that's a signal something is wrong (maybe journald config error, maybe tmpfs bind, maybe disk-full). Surface diagnostically.
Deploy step is idempotent. Re-running on a persistent-journal-already-working Pi is a no-op that still verifies.
Do NOT touch journald.conf.d content. The drop-in file itself is fine per Spool; the issue is systemd-journald not picking it up.
stopConditions: STOP if the verification check triggers on a clean-install Pi that has never been deployed before (machine-id subdir might not exist yet) -- make the first-run case graceful but still verify post-restart.
STOP if /var/log/journal existence requires SELinux or apparmor accommodation on some Pi OS versions -- surface in inbox; do not paper over.

STOP if the live-deploy acceptance test fails and the root cause is NOT systemd-journald restart (e.g., disk-full or permissions) -- file TD, complete story via deploy-gate only, leave real fix for Sprint 19.

stories.title: Server tier systemd unit -- mirror of US-210 for chi-srv-01
stories.intent: Chi-Srv-01 runs uvicorn src.server.main:app as a user-mode process under mcornelison (PID 3985160 per Spool's audit). No obd-server.service systemd unit. No Restart policy. No autostart on reboot. Any server reboot or process crash = server stays down until CIO manually restarts it; Pi sync fails silently post-reboot. This is the server-tier parallel of pre-US-210 Pi state. Fix: create + install deploy/obd-server.service with Restart=always + RestartSec=5 + After=network.target mariadb.service + User=mcornelison + EnvironmentFile for DB creds. Deploy step in deploy/deploy-server.sh installs + enables the unit. One-time cutover migrates currently-running uvicorn cleanly. Acceptance: server restarts on process-kill + on host-reboot.

filesToTouch: deploy/obd-server.service (new -- systemd unit; Restart=always; RestartSec=5; After=network.target mariadb.service; User=mcornelison; Type=simple; WorkingDirectory=/mnt/projects/O/OBD2v2; EnvironmentFile=/mnt/projects/O/OBD2v2/.env; ExecStart=/home/mcornelison/obd2-server-venv/bin/uvicorn src.server.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000)
deploy/deploy-server.sh (new step_install_server_unit: sync-if-changed pattern like US-210's eclipse-obd unit install; runs on every default deploy; handles the one-time cutover from manual uvicorn)
deploy/deploy-server.sh step_restart_server (update to use sudo systemctl restart obd-server instead of pkill + nohup uvicorn pattern -- cleaner, systemd manages lifecycle)
specs/architecture.md (Section 6 or new Section 'Server Tier Deployment': document obd-server.service unit + Restart policy + deploy flow)
docs/testing.md (Server systemd unit verification: post-deploy checks + recovery-from-crash test)
tests/deploy/test_obd_server_service.sh (new bash test; verifies via SSH that post-install obd-server.service is enabled + active + Restart=always)
offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-23-from-spool-post-deploy-system-test-findings.md (annotate with closure reference)
filesToRead: deploy/deploy-pi.sh step_install_eclipse_obd_unit (Sprint 16 post-deploy fix pattern to mirror for server)
deploy/deploy-server.sh current flow (find the right place to add the install step + restart step rewrite)
src/server/main.py (confirm uvicorn entry point + startup behavior)
doNotTouch: src/server/ application code (scope is systemd unit + deploy flow only)
MariaDB configuration (After= directive references it but does not manage it)
server .env file content (EnvironmentFile references it but this story does not change secrets)

groundingRefs.value: uvicorn runs as unmanaged user-mode process PID 3985160 under mcornelison, no systemd unit
Restart=always + RestartSec=5
After=network.target mariadb.service
groundingRefs.source: systemd best practice + server needs MariaDB up

groundingRefs.value: User=mcornelison (not root); EnvironmentFile for DB creds
groundingRefs.unit: security posture mirror from Pi tier

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: SSH chi-srv-01; confirm no obd-server.service in systemd; confirm uvicorn running as user-mode process; note its PID for cutover verification.
deploy/obd-server.service file created with correct unit content (Restart=always, Dependencies, User, WorkingDirectory, EnvironmentFile, ExecStart).
deploy-server.sh step_install_server_unit: sync-if-changed install to /etc/systemd/system/; daemon-reload; enable; runs on every default deploy (not just --init).
step_restart_server updated: uses sudo systemctl restart obd-server + 2s sleep + is-active check (replacing pkill+nohup pattern).

One-time cutover test: with current manual uvicorn running, deploy-server.sh cleanly stops the manual process and starts the systemd-managed one; `curl -s http://chi-srv-01:8000/api/v1/health` returns healthy within 10s.
Process-kill test: ssh chi-srv-01 + sudo kill -9 $(pidof uvicorn); within 10s, systemd restarts; health endpoint healthy.
Host-reboot test (if CIO willing): sudo reboot; after boot, obd-server.service starts automatically; health endpoint healthy.
specs/architecture.md + docs/testing.md updated.
Fast suite 0 regressions; shellcheck clean on deploy-server.sh; sprint_lint 0 errors.
Inbox confirmation to Spool: offices/tuner/inbox/YYYY-MM-DD-from-ralph-us231-server-unit-live.md with cutover PID before/after + restart-policy verification.
verification: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.10 'systemctl cat obd-server.service | head -20; systemctl is-active obd-server; systemctl is-enabled obd-server'
bash deploy/deploy-server.sh  (full deploy including new unit install + restart step)
ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.10 'sudo kill -9 $(pidof uvicorn) && sleep 5 && systemctl is-active obd-server'  (process-kill recovery test)
invariants: One-time cutover MUST be clean: manual uvicorn PID stopped before systemd-managed uvicorn started. No overlap, no port conflict.
Restart=always with StartLimitBurst cap (mirror US-210's 10/300s). Unbounded restart on a truly broken server is worse than the problem.
User=mcornelison (never root). WorkingDirectory is project root.
EnvironmentFile MUST use the existing .env on the server; do NOT hardcode secrets in the unit.
Do NOT change server application code. Unit wraps existing uvicorn entry; app is untouched.
Deploy step install-if-changed pattern matches Sprint 16 Pi-side fix (cmp -s + daemon-reload only when content differs).
stopConditions: STOP if /mnt/projects/O/OBD2v2/.env doesn't exist or is empty -- server won't start without DB creds; file inbox note before creating empty unit.
STOP if existing manual uvicorn is bound to a port/config that conflicts with the unit's ExecStart -- reconcile before cutover.
STOP if systemd unit install needs SELinux context adjustments on whatever Debian flavor chi-srv-01 runs -- surface in inbox.
STOP if the one-time cutover causes a health-check gap > 30s -- not acceptable; investigate ordering.

stories.title: TD-035 close -- retry loop responds to SIGTERM within 2s
stories.intent: Close TD-035 (filed 2026-04-23 Sprint 17 post-deploy debug). systemctl restart eclipse-obd takes ~90s during car-off because the collector's BT retry loop uses time.sleep(backoff) which doesn't wake on SIGTERM. Systemd ends up SIGKILLing after TimeoutStopSec. Service ends up in failed state requiring systemctl reset-failed. Bigger downstream risk: if US-216's TRIGGER stage sends SIGTERM for graceful shutdown and collector is in retry sleep, the 90s wait may overlap with actual power loss. Fix: replace time.sleep with threading.Event.wait + SIGTERM handler sets the event. Symmetric with how US-211 ReconnectLoop should also handle shutdown signals.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/obd_connection.py::connect (retry loop: replace time.sleep(backoff) with self._shutdownEvent.wait(timeout=backoff); add self._shutdownEvent threading.Event attribute)
src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py (US-211 artifact: apply same event.wait pattern to its backoff; ensure consistent shutdown plumbing)
src/pi/main.py (main signal handler: on SIGTERM/SIGINT, set the shutdown event on all registered components)
src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (plumb the shutdown_event through orchestrator -> obd_connection + reconnect_loop)
tests/pi/obdii/test_sigterm_responsiveness.py (new -- mock SIGTERM mid-retry; assert process-equivalent loop exits within 2s)
tests/pi/obdii/test_reconnect_loop_sigterm.py (new -- US-211 ReconnectLoop wakes on event.set())
offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-035-collector-bt-retry-ignores-sigterm.md (annotate Closed with reference to US-232)
doNotTouch: Backoff schedule values [1, 5, 10, 30, 60] stays (US-211 invariant).
Error classification (3-bucket stays per US-211).
systemd TimeoutStopSec (leave at default; fix is application-side, not service-side).

groundingRefs.value: Target: SIGTERM-to-exit within 2 seconds (from current ~90s SIGKILL)
Replace time.sleep(N) with threading.Event.wait(timeout=N)
Main thread SIGTERM handler sets event; worker thread wakes from wait()

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg --files-with-matches 'time.sleep|signal\.signal' src/pi/ to map current sleep sites + signal handling.

src/pi/obdii/obd_connection.py::connect: retry loop uses shutdownEvent.wait(timeout=backoff) instead of time.sleep. On event set, raises a clean Shutdown exception (or returns a sentinel) to exit cleanly.
src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py (US-211): same event-based wait for backoff.
src/pi/main.py: SIGTERM + SIGINT signal handlers set the shutdown event for all wired components.
Orchestrator shutdown path: on receiving shutdown signal, cascades to stop connection + drive detector + sync client + orchestrator runLoop.
Test: mock SIGTERM mid-retry; process-equivalent loop exits within 2s (with a 5s-tolerance safety margin for CI flakiness).
Test: US-211 ReconnectLoop test verifies same event-wake semantics.
TD-035 file annotated Closed with closing story reference.
Fast suite 0 regressions; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.
Real-world verification: ssh Pi + sudo systemctl restart eclipse-obd during car-off (BT retry state); restart completes within 10s (down from ~90s).
verification: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'time sudo systemctl restart eclipse-obd'  (expect <10s)
invariants: Backoff schedule values stay [1, 5, 10, 30, 60] -- not retuning.
Shutdown event is one-shot: once set, never cleared (process is exiting).
Do NOT add signal handlers in worker threads -- Python signal module is main-thread-only.
Cascading shutdown: ordered stop (connection first, then detector, then sync, then orchestrator). Components each have their own event reference.
Normal timeout expiry (no event set) returns False or None; event-triggered wake returns True -- caller distinguishes.
stopConditions: STOP if main thread doesn't currently install signal handlers (Python subprocess or daemon thread context) -- surface; may need different signal pattern.
STOP if US-211's reconnect_loop.py has internal sleep in a subthread that's harder to interrupt than the main retry loop -- scope-split into a separate TD.
STOP if real-world restart test still exceeds 10s after fix -- diagnose (may be other sleep sites elsewhere in the collector that also need conversion).

stories.title: Pre-mint orphan-row backfill -- 225 Drive 3 rows with NULL drive_id from BT-connect window
stories.intent: Drive 3 has 225 rows tagged data_source='real' with NULL drive_id from the 39-second BT-connect-to-drive-start window. The collector was connected to the car and writing rows, but the engine-state machine hadn't yet fired the CRANKING transition that mints the drive_id. Drive 3's drive_id was minted only after engine started. Similar to Session 23's pre-US-200 NULL-drive_id issue but narrower (39s window vs 2+ min). Ralph shipped a backfill for the older data in Sprint 15; reuse that pattern + apply to Drive 3's pre-mint orphans OR extend the engine_state machine to cover the BT-connect-before-cranking window with a distinct drive_id (or NULL drive_id by policy, documented). Low urgency -- doesn't block anything, just keeps drive-scoped queries tidy.

filesToTouch: scripts/backfill_premint_orphans.py (new OR extend existing backfill script -- match pre-mint orphan rows to the subsequent drive's drive_id IF window < configurable threshold; --dry-run + --execute flags; idempotent)

src/pi/obdii/engine_state.py (decide policy: (a) backfill via script after-the-fact, (b) pre-emit a provisional drive_id at BT-connect that gets reconciled at CRANKING, (c) leave NULL and document as authoritative policy)
specs/architecture.md Section 5 Drive Lifecycle (document the pre-mint orphan policy explicitly)
docs/testing.md (document the backfill script + how to run it post-drive)
tests/pi/obdii/test_premint_orphan_backfill.py (new -- synthesize pre-mint rows + subsequent drive; assert backfill associates them correctly OR assert NULL policy holds per Ralph's choice)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/engine_state.py (US-200 state machine + drive_id minting timing)
scripts/truncate_session23.py + prior-Sprint backfill if any exists (patterns to reuse)
doNotTouch: Drive 3's non-NULL drive_id rows (225 orphans are the scope; 3272 real-drive rows stay associated)
Mode 01 PID polling (capture continues as-is during BT-connect-before-cranking window)
Regression fixture eclipse_idle.db

groundingRefs.value: Drive 3: 225 rows with data_source='real' AND drive_id IS NULL in 39-second BT-connect window
BT-connect fires before CRANKING transition; drive_id minted at CRANKING only
Three options: (a) backfill script, (b) provisional drive_id at BT-connect, (c) document NULL as policy

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: SSH Pi; verify the 225 pre-mint orphan row count for Drive 3 (or whatever Drive exists post-US-227 truncate).
Policy decision documented in completionNotes: (a), (b), or (c) with rationale.
If (a) backfill script: scripts/backfill_premint_orphans.py implements the match logic + idempotent + dry-run gate.
If (b) provisional drive_id: engine_state.py + drive_id.py updated to mint provisional ID at BT-connect that gets reconciled at CRANKING (with policy for how to handle mid-window disconnect).
If (c) NULL policy: specs/architecture.md Section 5 documents the authoritative policy + tests assert NULL is preserved.
Tests cover the chosen strategy.
If applied retroactively (option a), post-run SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE drive_id IS NULL AND data_source='real' returns only the genuinely-ambiguous rows (or zero if full backfill).
Fast suite + new tests 0 regressions; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.
invariants: If backfill is the chosen strategy, it MUST be idempotent. Re-running on already-backfilled data is a no-op.
Matching window has a hard cap (e.g., 2 minutes). Orphans older than the cap stay NULL -- do not associate with a drive that started much later.
Do NOT retroactively change drive_summary row ownership (the drive_summary's drive_id is authoritative).
Do NOT use this script/policy to retrofit Session 23's 149 rows -- those are the regression fixture and stay frozen.
stopConditions: STOP if the policy decision requires changes to US-200 engine_state transitions that affect other stories -- surface to PM.
STOP if the 39-second window is revealed to be much longer in other observed drives (e.g., > 2 min on a drive where the car was off but Pi stayed connected) -- reassess the matching cap.
STOP if provisional drive_id approach breaks US-219 review ritual or US-214 dual-writer reconciliation -- verify compatibility.
